content_of_post_to_comment: Look. When I get off my phone for the first time in many hours or days. Reality just Hits so hard like. I see people on their phones quite a lot. See people trying to talk to people but they immediately pull out their phone on them. Its crazy. I tried to talk to someone in my school and they done pulled out their phone and acted like I was not talking to them. Dawg. The people who I don't like or don't really have connection with literally it's pretty much stuck in the infinite Loop of looking around with people that have phones. But the only light I see in them is that they still try to talk to people no matter what. That's not the worst part. When I get off my phone when I wanted to and I try to do something peak or try to look for something else to do. There was nothing.. and as soon as you know it I faded back into the security of the internet. The internet ain't even that great neither. YouTube comments or whatever or just randomly toxic (pretty common I know) but dude I feel like they just gotten worse. Random commas calling people robots or NPCs or real people like. Where did all of that come from. What happened to comments being just comments. Since when are we calling random comments that look normal robots. Like sure in the early stages of when the internet was starting to fall off there were comments that didn't make any sense so they were probably robots but like. It gotten so insane that people who literally like copy people's comments or whatever just get called or something just because they have the same comment as someone else. Like dude there are other things that you could be doing other than harassing people's comments. Plus most of the quality of the comments are pretty bad. Some are just so repetitive some of them are so heartless. It's almost like I'm looking at a whole entire void. Don't get me started on the creativity. Look there was a lot of animators back then in 2010 - 2020 (older or recently) everything was pretty much fine until brain rot was everywhere. And it just got so bad I had to clear out my whole entire watch History or whatever Justice start a new clean slate of actual content for me to watch but then a few more years later now content is like soulless and repetitive just like the comments on any media. Scariest part people had not even noticed one bit about any of it and they just mindlessly scroll and just indulge in anything that not anything that would be a potential Peak viral thing or whatever. (There is more I want to say but I'm to lazy) {Hopefully my grammer is ok}
comment: I’ve noticed something similar after taking breaks from my phone: the hardest part isn’t missing the apps, it’s realizing how little space there is between boredom and reaching for them again. I also think the internet didn’t just get worse, it got optimized for the fastest reactions instead of curiosity. Makes you wonder how much of our attention we’re giving away without noticing.
